LAW AND MOTION, DEPT. 302, PLAINTIFF ROSA OROZCO'S MOTION TO FILE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT FOR PURPOSE OF RECLASSIFICATION TO UNLIMITED JURISDICTION. THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: PLAINTIFF ROSA OROZCO'S MOTION FOR LEAVE TO FILE A FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT AND RESULTING RECLASSIFICATION OF THIS CASE TO UNLIMITED JURISDICTION IS GRANTED. NO OPPOSITION FILED. ONCE THE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS FILED, THE CASE WILL BE UNLIMITED JURISDICTION. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN, CLERK: E. LARNAUTI, REPORTER: KELLEY DEYOUNG, CSR 6780 (KDEYOUNG@SFTC.ORG) (302/HEK)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To File First Amended Complaint For Purpose Of Reclassification To Unlimited Jurisdiction
Off Calendar Apr-05-2017 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFF ROSA OROZCO'S MOTION TO FILE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT FOR PURPOSE OF RECLASSIFICATION TO UNLIMITED JURISDICTION.

No appearances. The Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: Plaintiff Rosa Orozco's motion for leave to file a first amended complaint and resulting reclassification of this case to unlimited jurisdiction is granted. No opposition filed. Once the first amended complaint is filed, the case will be unlimited jurisdiction. Order signed. Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Reporter: Kelley DeYoung, CSR 6780 (kdeyoung@sftc.org) (302/HEK)
